role_title: Product Manager - Analytics, AI Private Markets

level_signals: 5+ years combined experience in a product management role
Expected to exercise considerable latitude in determining objectives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years combined experience in a product management role
Proven track record of delivering impactful product releases to an roadmap
Experience in database management, machine learning, AI, data analysis, data modeling, data analysis, data analysis, and business intelligence
Experience using JIRA or other agile development tools
Private Markets: a strong understanding of in private markets investing
Financial Concepts: knowledge of financial modeling, valuation, and key performance indicators (KPIs) used in the private markets is important.
Ability to travel as required for client engagements (-20%), including internationally
Highly polished executive presence and sophistication to gain credibility with the firm's most senior professionals and provide leadership, guidance, and direction to a team.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Preferred background to include enterprise SaaS within data management, data science, and / or artificial intelligence

Experience working with Microsoft Power BI, (Power Query, PerformancePoint, PowerView a plus), Power Platform, Analytics (Synapse, backend data modeling in support of Analytics), SSRS, SSAS, Excel Services, IPaaS, Snowflake preferred
Investment Process: familiarity with the investment process, including deal sourcing, due diligence, and portfolio management, can also be helpful.

scope.surface: Core data management, analytics, reporting, and AI solutions for Charles River Private Markets platform (cloud-based data and analytics platform)
scope.business_model: enterprise SaaS, B2B, financial services / asset management
core_responsibilities: Own core data ingestion, data management, analytics, reporting solutions/services and roadmap
Oversee user experience of the facilitated end-to-end process from data ingestion to normalization to data accessibility and delivery
Ensure client experience is simple, intuitive, and drives measured adoption
Monitor metrics against platform performance and service levels
Identify, assemble and coordinate internal and third-party resources
Create and maintain comprehensive product documentation
Develop a detailed project plan to monitor and track progress
Work across Professional Services, Product, and Engineering team to prioritize bugs, defects, and enhancements
Provide timely updates on key roadmap updates to senior management

green_flags: Clear product ownership scope described (data ingestion through analytics delivery)
Described as open, supportive, inclusive environment
State Street is a large, established financial institution with significant resources
Explicit mention of flexible work program and comprehensive benefits
red_flags: Compensation range is wide ($80K-$140K), leaving seniority level ambiguous
JD body does not clarify remote eligibility; location type inferred as onsite based on Burlington, MA city listing
JD text contains repeated phrases ('data analysis' listed three times) suggesting possible copy-paste errors and lower editorial quality
Travel requirement of ~20% including internationally may be a constraint for some candidates
Private markets domain knowledge listed under 'Technical Skills' and 'Domain Knowledge' simultaneously, indicating potential role scope ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + comp range)
location.type (inferred as onsite from city listing; no explicit remote/hybrid language in JD body)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: Location flexibility not stated — JD lists Burlington, Massachusetts as primary location but mentions 'flexible Work Program'; unclear if hybrid or fully onsite is expected. Treated as onsite.

Private markets domain knowledge appears as both a 'required' implied item under domain knowledge and as 'preferred' in technical skills section — treated as hard given unconditional framing in domain knowledge section.
Travel percentage noted as '-20%' in JD — likely a typo for '~20%'.
Salary range is very wide ($80K-$140K), making it difficult to pinpoint seniority band precisely.
